description_long: This is a single-bladed axe featuring a broad, double-edged steel blade with a matte gray finish. The blade is engraved with the name 'Joseph Hart' in light blue, which stands out against the metallic surface. The handle is made of wood, displaying a natural brown color and a pronounced curvature, likely designed for ergonomic grip and efficient swinging. The blade is securely mounted to the handle using a traditional wedged style, with the top of the handle visible above the blade. The overall design is utilitarian, with no additional decorative elements aside from the engraved name. The axe appears robust and suitable for heavy-duty tasks, such as chopping wood or demolition work in an urban environment.
description: A robust, double-edged steel axe with a curved wooden handle and the name 'Joseph Hart' engraved on the blade. The design is utilitarian and sturdy, suitable for heavy-duty urban tasks.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the double-edged blade and the engraved name 'Joseph Hart', with the curved wooden handle visible below.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thin profile of the blade and the curvature of the wooden handle, with the blade's thickness and mounting visible.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the opposite side of the blade and handle, with the engraved name still visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the blade's thin profile and the handle's curvature, with the mounting style visible at the top.
attribute_handle_type: Curved wooden handle with a natural finish, ergonomic grip, and oval cross-section.
attribute_blade_design: Broad, double-edged steel blade with a symmetrical profile and no beard or poll, featuring an engraved name.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with the handle passing through the blade's eye.
